title: Automated Tissue Fixation with 10% NBF (Leica)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aa1m0","text":"Human tissue specimens are frequently formalin-fixed and paraffin-embedded (FFPE) for histological staining,  pathological review, and archival purposes. This is the routine fixation protocol used by the Specialized Histopathology (SHP) Core and the Department of Pathology at Brigham and Women's Hospital. The protocol uses the Leica ASP300 S Fully Enclosed Tissue Processor to automate fixation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1vkc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ipq4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7c5k5","text":"When fixatives other than 10% NBF are used, the case will be discussed with the investigator by management to ensure the best possible result.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"e2e8l","text":"Fixation times can range from 8 to 72 hrs depending on size, thickness, and type of the sample. It is not recommended to fix the sample for longer time periods, as this can have deleterious effects for various assays.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"72b92","text":"Materials \u0026 Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3l727","text":"Leica ASP300 S Fully Enclosed Tissue Processor","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bb7js","text":"10% Neutral buffered formalin","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a1gh4","text":"Ethyl Alcohol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b0av2","text":"Xylene","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b3m43","text":"Paraffin wax","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Formalin Short Program A B C D 1 Station/Reagent Time Temp P/V 2 10% NBF 00:12 min 40 Y 3 70% Ethyl Alcohol 00:15 min 40 Y 4 95% Ethyl Alcohol 00:15 min 40 Y 5 95% Ethyl Alcohol 00:20 min 40 Y 6 100% Ethyl Alcohol 00:20 min 40 Y 7 100% Ethyl Alcohol 00:20 min 40 Y 8 Xylene 00:20 min 40 Y 9 Xylene 00:30 min 40 Y 10 Paraffin Wax 1 00:05 min 60 Y 11 Paraffin Wax 2 00:10 min 60 Y 12 Paraffin Wax 3 00:15 min 60 Y Table 1. Formalin short program.
Program the Leica ASP300 S Fully Enclosed Tissue Processor with either the short program or long program depending on size, thickness, and type of sample.

Formalin Long Program A B C D 1 Station/Reagent Time Temp P/V 2 10% NBF 02:00 hr 40 Y 3 70% Ethyl Alcohol 00:45 min 40 Y 4 95% Ethyl Alcohol 00:45 min 40 Y 5 95% Ethyl Alcohol 00:45 min 40 Y 6 100% Ethyl Alcohol 00:30 min 40 Y 7 100% Ethyl Alcohol 01:00 min 40 Y 8 100% Ethyl Alcohol 30 min 40 Y 9 Xylene 30 min 40 Y 10 Xylene 30 min 40 Y 11 Paraffin Wax 1 30 min 60 Y 12 Paraffin Wax 2 30 min 60 Y 13 Paraffin Wax 3 30 min 60 Y Table 2. Formalin long program.
